IsDebuggerPresent.description: Checks the BeingDebugged flag of the PEB. The most commonly used debugger detection API -- trivially bypassed but ubiquitous in malware.

CheckRemoteDebuggerPresent.description: Checks whether a debugger is attached to the current process from another process. Internally calls NtQueryInformationProcess(ProcessDebugPort).

NtQueryInformationProcess.description: Native NT API queried with ProcessDebugPort (0x7), ProcessDebugFlags (0x1F), or ProcessDebugObjectHandle (0x1E) to detect an attached debugger -- returns -1, 0, or a non-null handle respectively when a debugger is present. Also used for parent process checks (ProcessBasicInformation).

RtlQueryProcessHeapInformation.description: Reads the heap flags from the current process memory. When a process is started under a debugger, the Flags and ForceFlags fields of the heap header are set to abnormal values (HEAP_TAIL_CHECKING_ENABLED, HEAP_FREE_CHECKING_ENABLED, HEAP_VALIDATE_PARAMETERS_ENABLED).

RtlQueryProcessDebugInformation.description: Reads process debug information including heap flags. Equivalent to RtlQueryProcessHeapInformation but queries a specific process by PID via PDI_HEAPS | PDI_HEAP_BLOCKS.

NtQuerySystemInformation.description: Called with SystemKernelDebuggerInformation (0x23) to detect a kernel debugger. Returns DebuggerEnabled=1 and DebuggerNotPresent=0 when a kernel debugger is attached to the system.

NtQueryObject.description: Called with ObjectAllTypesInformation to enumerate all kernel object types and count DebugObject handles. A non-zero TotalNumberOfObjects for DebugObject indicates a debug session is or was active on the system.

NtClose.description: Native variant of CloseHandle. Passing an invalid handle (e.g. 0xDEADBEEF) raises EXCEPTION_INVALID_HANDLE (0xC0000008) under a debugger instead of silently failing -- revealing debugger presence via the exception. Used directly to bypass hooks on kernel32!CloseHandle.

CloseHandle.description: Passing an invalid handle (e.g. 0xDEADBEEF) raises EXCEPTION_INVALID_HANDLE (0xC0000008) under a debugger, while it silently fails without one. The exception caught by an SEH handler confirms debugger presence.

HeapWalk.description: Iterates heap entries to check if the tail byte sequence 0xABABABAB is present (appended when HEAP_TAIL_CHECKING_ENABLED is set in NtGlobalFlag). This flag is set when a process is started under a debugger.

DebugBreak.description: Triggers a breakpoint exception (EXCEPTION_BREAKPOINT). Without a debugger, the exception is forwarded to the SEH handler; with a debugger, it is consumed. Used as a reliable SEH-based debugger check.

GetThreadContext.description: Retrieves the thread context with CONTEXT_DEBUG_REGISTERS to inspect hardware debug registers DR0-DR3. Non-zero values indicate hardware breakpoints have been set by a debugger.

GetCurrentThread.description: Returns a pseudo-handle to the current thread. Meaningful only in combination with GetThreadContext for hardware breakpoint detection via DR0-DR3.

OpenProcess.description: Called on csrss.exe via CsrGetProcessId -- the call succeeds only if the user has SeDebugPrivilege, which is granted when a debugger is attached. Also used to open other processes for cross-process memory reads (function patch detection).

ReadProcessMemory.description: Used to read the first bytes of kernel32!IsDebuggerPresent in another process and compare them to the current process. A difference reveals that the function was patched or hooked by a debugger.

WriteProcessMemory.description: Used in Anti-Step-Over: patches the byte at the return address with 0x90 (NOP) if it is 0xCC (INT3 breakpoint). Meaningful only in combination with VirtualProtect.

VirtualAlloc.description: Too generic alone. Meaningful in combination with VirtualProtect for guard page memory breakpoint detection, or with GetWriteWatch for write-watch based debugger detection.

VirtualProtect.description: Too generic alone. Meaningful in combination with WriteProcessMemory (Anti-Step-Over), with VirtualAlloc for guard page tricks, or for patching ntdll!DbgBreakPoint/DbgUiRemoteBreakin.

GetWriteWatch.description: Called on a MEM_WRITE_WATCH region to detect unexpected memory writes by a debugger or API hook. More writes than expected indicate debugger instrumentation probing the buffer.

Toolhelp32ReadProcessMemory.description: Reads a byte at the return address of the calling function. If the byte is 0xCC (INT3), a step-over breakpoint is present. Meaningful only in combination with VirtualProtect.

NtQueryVirtualMemory.description: Called with MemoryWorkingSetList to retrieve the Working Set of the current process. Checks the Shared and ShareCount fields for code pages -- software breakpoints cause the page to become private (non-shared), revealing their presence.

SetUnhandledExceptionFilter.description: Registers a custom unhandled exception filter. Under a debugger, the filter is never called -- the exception is intercepted by the debugger instead. If the filter is reached, no debugger is present.

RaiseException.description: Called with DBG_CONTROL_C (0x40010005) or DBG_RIPEVENT (0x40010007) -- exceptions consumed by a debugger and not forwarded to the process exception handler. If the SEH handler is not reached, a debugger is present.

AddVectoredExceptionHandler.description: Registers a Vectored Exception Handler (VEH). Used to count hardware breakpoint single-step exceptions in an instruction sequence, or to chain exception handlers to hide the control flow from static analysis.

RemoveVectoredExceptionHandler.description: Meaningful only in combination with AddVectoredExceptionHandler -- used to chain VEH handlers for control flow obfuscation or instruction counting anti-debug.

NtSetInformationThread.description: Called with ThreadHideFromDebugger (0x11) to detach the current thread from the debugger. The thread continues running but the debugger stops receiving events -- a breakpoint in the hidden thread crashes the process instead of breaking in.

DebugActiveProcess.description: Used in self-debugging anti-attach: the process spawns a child which attempts to attach as debugger to the parent via DebugActiveProcess. Failure reveals another debugger is already attached. Meaningful only in combination with CreateProcess.

CreateProcess.description: Too generic alone. Meaningful only in combination with DebugActiveProcess for self-debugging anti-attach detection.

DbgSetDebugFilterState.description: Wraps ntdll!NtSetDebugFilterState. Succeeds only when a kernel-mode debugger is present or as a side-effect of some user-mode debuggers. Requires administrator privileges.

NtSetDebugFilterState.description: Native variant of DbgSetDebugFilterState. Returns STATUS_SUCCESS only when a kernel debugger is present or due to side-effects of certain user-mode debuggers.

SwitchToThread.description: Returns 0 when no thread context switch can occur -- which happens when single-stepping in a debugger. A counter incremented on zero returns reveals tracing. Meaningful only in combination with NtYieldExecution.

NtYieldExecution.description: Returns STATUS_NO_YIELD_PERFORMED (0x40000024) when single-stepping under a debugger prevents a context switch. Meaningful only in combination with SwitchToThread.

FindWindowA.description: Searches for top-level windows with known debugger class names: OLLYDBG, WinDbgFrameClass, ID (Immunity Debugger), ObsidianGUI, Qt5QWindowIcon, Zeta Debugger, Rock Debugger, x64dbg.

FindWindowW.description: Unicode variant of FindWindowA. Searches for top-level windows with known debugger class names to detect running debugger instances.

FindWindowExA.description: Extended variant of FindWindowA allowing child window search. Used to detect debugger windows by class name when the top-level window search is insufficient.

FindWindowExW.description: Unicode extended variant of FindWindowA. Used to detect debugger windows by class name.

GetShellWindow.description: Retrieves the handle of the shell window (explorer.exe). Meaningful only in combination with GetWindowThreadProcessId and NtQueryInformationProcess for parent process PID comparison.

GetWindowThreadProcessId.description: Extracts the PID of the shell process (explorer.exe) from its window handle. Meaningful only in combination with GetShellWindow and NtQueryInformationProcess for parent process checks.

OutputDebugStringA.description: Pre-Vista: if a debugger is present it consumes the DBG_PRINTEXCEPTION_C event, preventing GetLastError from being overwritten. Meaningful only in combination with SetLastError and GetLastError.

SetLastError.description: Sets a known error code before OutputDebugStringA. If GetLastError returns a different value after the call, no debugger consumed the event. Meaningful only in combination with OutputDebugStringA.

GetLastError.description: Checks whether the error code was overwritten after OutputDebugStringA. Meaningful only in combination with SetLastError and OutputDebugStringA.

GetLocalTime.description: Used in timing-based anti-debug: elapsed time between two GetLocalTime calls is compared against a threshold -- single-stepping in a debugger introduces measurable delay. Meaningful only in combination.

GetSystemTime.description: Used in timing-based anti-debug: elapsed time between two GetSystemTime calls is compared against a threshold. Meaningful only in combination.

GetTickCount.description: Used in timing-based anti-debug: measures elapsed milliseconds between two calls to detect single-stepping. Meaningful only in combination.

QueryPerformanceCounter.description: High-resolution timer used to measure elapsed ticks between two calls. Single-stepping in a debugger introduces measurable delay. Meaningful only in combination.

timeGetTime.description: Multimedia timer used to measure elapsed milliseconds between two calls for timing-based debugger detection. Meaningful only in combination.

LoadLibraryA.description: Used in the LOAD_DLL_DEBUG_EVENT handle leak trick: a DLL is loaded and then opened with exclusive access via CreateFileA. If the open fails, the debugger did not close the LOAD_DLL_DEBUG_INFO handle. Meaningful only in combination with CreateFileA.

LoadLibraryW.description: Unicode variant of LoadLibraryA. Used in the same LOAD_DLL_DEBUG_EVENT handle leak detection trick. Meaningful only in combination with CreateFileW.

CreateFileA.description: Used in two detection tricks: (1) exclusively opening the current process binary -- fails if the debugger holds an unclosed CREATE_PROCESS_DEBUG_EVENT handle; (2) exclusively opening a DLL loaded via LoadLibraryA to detect an unclosed LOAD_DLL_DEBUG_EVENT handle.

CreateFileW.description: Unicode variant of CreateFileA. Used in the same process binary and DLL handle leak detection tricks.

OLLYDBG.description: OllyDbg window class name. Searched via FindWindowA to detect a running OllyDbg instance.

WinDbgFrameClass.description: WinDbg window class name. Searched via FindWindowA to detect a running WinDbg instance.

ID.description: Immunity Debugger window class name. Searched via FindWindowA to detect a running Immunity Debugger instance. Short string -- confirm with FindWindowA import.

ObsidianGUI.description: Obsidian GUI debugger window class name. Searched via FindWindowA to detect this debugger.

Qt5QWindowIcon.description: Qt5-based debugger window class name (x64dbg, PE-bear, and others). Searched via FindWindowA to detect Qt-based reverse engineering tools.

Zeta Debugger.description: Zeta Debugger window class name. Searched via FindWindowA to detect this debugger.

Rock Debugger.description: Rock Debugger window class name. Searched via FindWindowA to detect this debugger.

antidbg.description: Window class name associated with anti-debug test tools. Presence signals a debugging or analysis environment.

x64dbg.description: x64dbg window title or process name. Searched via FindWindowA or process enumeration to detect x64dbg.

ollydbg.exe.description: OllyDbg process name. Used in process enumeration (Toolhelp32) to detect a running OllyDbg instance.

x64dbg.exe.description: x64dbg process name. Used in process enumeration to detect a running x64dbg instance.

x32dbg.exe.description: x32dbg (32-bit variant of x64dbg) process name. Used in process enumeration to detect a running x32dbg instance.

windbg.exe.description: WinDbg process name. Used in process enumeration to detect a running WinDbg instance.

idag.exe.description: IDA Pro (32-bit) process name. Used in process enumeration to detect IDA Pro running.

idag64.exe.description: IDA Pro (64-bit) process name. Used in process enumeration to detect IDA Pro running.

idaw.exe.description: IDA Pro GUI process name (older versions). Used in process enumeration.

idaw64.exe.description: IDA Pro 64-bit GUI process name (older versions). Used in process enumeration.

immunitydebugger.exe.description: Immunity Debugger process name. Used in process enumeration to detect Immunity Debugger.

petools.exe.description: PE Tools process name. Used in process enumeration to detect PE analysis tools.

lordpe.exe.description: LordPE process name. Used in process enumeration to detect this PE dumper/editor.

ProcessHacker.exe.description: Process Hacker process name. Used in process enumeration to detect this advanced process monitor.

dbgview.exe.description: Sysinternals DebugView process name. Its presence suggests an active debugging or monitoring session.

ThreadHideFromDebugger.description: String associated with NtSetInformationThread ThreadHideFromDebugger class. Its presence as a literal string suggests dynamic API resolution of this anti-debug technique.

IsDebuggerPresent.description: String used to resolve IsDebuggerPresent via GetProcAddress at runtime, bypassing static import detection.

CheckRemoteDebuggerPresent.description: String used to resolve CheckRemoteDebuggerPresent via GetProcAddress at runtime, bypassing static import detection.

NtQueryInformationProcess.description: String used to resolve NtQueryInformationProcess from ntdll.dll via GetProcAddress at runtime to evade static import analysis.

ntdll.dll.description: ntdll.dll is loaded via LoadLibraryA to dynamically resolve native NT APIs (NtQueryInformationProcess, NtClose, etc.) that bypass hooks on Win32 equivalents. Also a known window class name in some analysis environments.

DbgBreakPoint.description: Name of ntdll!DbgBreakPoint resolved via GetProcAddress to patch it with RET (0xC3), preventing a debugger from gaining control when attaching to the process.

DbgUiRemoteBreakin.description: Name of ntdll!DbgUiRemoteBreakin resolved via GetProcAddress to patch it with a TerminateProcess call, killing the process when a debugger attempts to attach.

CsrGetProcessId.description: Undocumented ntdll export resolved via GetProcAddress to get the PID of csrss.exe. Used to call OpenProcess on csrss.exe -- succeeds only with SeDebugPrivilege (i.e. under a debugger).

peb_beingdebugged_x86.description: MOV EAX, FS:[0x30] -- reads the PEB pointer on x86 to check the BeingDebugged flag at offset +0x2, bypassing IsDebuggerPresent hooks.

peb_beingdebugged_x64.description: MOV RAX, GS:[0x60] -- reads the PEB pointer on x64 to check the BeingDebugged flag at offset +0x2, bypassing IsDebuggerPresent hooks.

ntglobalflag_x86.description: MOV AL, [EAX+0x68] / AND AL, 0x70 / CMP AL, 0x70 -- reads NtGlobalFlag from PEB on x86 and checks for debugger heap flags (FLG_HEAP_ENABLE_TAIL_CHECK | FLG_HEAP_ENABLE_FREE_CHECK | FLG_HEAP_VALIDATE_PARAMETERS = 0x70).

ntglobalflag_x64.description: MOV AL, [RAX+0xBC] / AND AL, 0x70 / CMP AL, 0x70 -- reads NtGlobalFlag from PEB on x64 and checks for debugger heap flags (0x70).

kuser_shared_data_x86.description: MOV AL, [0x7FFE02D4] -- reads KdDebuggerEnabled from the KUSER_SHARED_DATA structure at its fixed user-mode address on x86 to detect a kernel debugger.

kuser_shared_data_x64.description: MOVZX EAX, BYTE PTR [0x7FFE02D4] -- reads KdDebuggerEnabled from KUSER_SHARED_DATA at its fixed address on x64 to detect a kernel debugger.

rdtsc_timing.description: RDTSC -- measures elapsed CPU cycles between two calls. Single-stepping in a debugger introduces measurable lag between two RDTSC reads, revealing the debugger presence.

int2ah_timing.description: INT 2Ah (KiGetTickCount syscall) -- kernel-mode tick count read via software interrupt. Used for timing-based anti-debug as an alternative to RDTSC, harder to hook.

int3_short.description: INT 3 (1-byte opcode 0xCC) -- standard software breakpoint. Used in SEH-based anti-debug: without a debugger the EXCEPTION_BREAKPOINT is forwarded to the exception handler; with a debugger it is consumed.

int3_long.description: INT 3 long form (2-byte opcode CD 03). Under a debugger the exception is consumed and EIP points mid-instruction; the misalignment can be exploited to detect tracing or corrupt the debugger's EIP.

int2d.description: INT 2Dh -- under a debugger this exception is consumed and the byte following INT 2Dh is skipped, causing disassembly desynchronization. A NOP placed after INT 2Dh is skipped under a debugger, altering the execution path.

ice_undocumented.description: ICE -- Intel undocumented instruction (opcode 0xF1). Raises EXCEPTION_SINGLE_STEP (0x80000004). Under a debugger in trace mode, this exception is treated as a normal single-step and the handler is not called, revealing tracing.

popf_trap_flag.description: PUSHFD / ADD DWORD PTR [ESP], 0x100 / POPFD -- sets the Trap Flag in EFLAGS. Raises EXCEPTION_SINGLE_STEP; under a debugger the flag is cleared before delivery so the exception handler is never reached, revealing the debugger.

rep_prefix_anti_trace.description: REP prefix (0xF3) + FS segment override (0x64) + ICE (0xF1). Some debuggers (OllyDbg-like) skip the prefix and jump directly to the ICE instruction, altering control flow compared to non-debugged execution.

pushss_popss_pushf.description: PUSH SS / POP SS / PUSHF -- after single-stepping through this sequence in a debugger, the Trap Flag is set in the pushed EFLAGS value and remains detectable. The debugger normally clears TF after each event but the push captures it before clearing.

software_bp_scan_cc.description: CMP BYTE PTR [reg], 0xCC -- checks a memory byte against the INT3 opcode. Used to scan function prologues for software breakpoints placed by the debugger.

hardware_bp_dr0_check_x86.description: MOV EAX, FS:[0x18] -- reads the TEB pointer on x86 as part of GetThreadContext-free DR register access to detect hardware breakpoints in DR0-DR3.

combinations.name: Hardware breakpoint detection
combinations.description: Retrieves the current thread context with CONTEXT_DEBUG_REGISTERS and checks DR0-DR3. A non-zero value in any debug register signals that a hardware breakpoint has been set by an attached debugger.

combinations.name: Timing-based anti-debug (GetTickCount)
combinations.description: Measures elapsed milliseconds between two GetTickCount calls around sensitive code. Excessive delay caused by single-stepping or breakpoints in a debugger exceeds the expected threshold.

combinations.name: Timing-based anti-debug (QueryPerformanceCounter)
combinations.description: Measures elapsed high-resolution ticks between two QueryPerformanceCounter calls. Debugger single-stepping introduces measurable lag that exceeds normal execution delta.

combinations.name: Timing-based anti-debug (GetLocalTime)
combinations.description: Measures wall-clock time between two GetLocalTime calls. Coarse-grained timing check used to detect slow execution caused by debugger tracing.

combinations.name: Anti-Step-Over (WriteProcessMemory)
combinations.description: Checks the byte at the function's return address for 0xCC (INT3 step-over breakpoint) and patches it with 0x90 (NOP) using WriteProcessMemory after making it writable with VirtualProtect. Breaks the debugger's step-over or causes a crash.

combinations.name: Anti-Step-Over (Toolhelp32ReadProcessMemory)
combinations.description: Reads the byte at the return address via Toolhelp32ReadProcessMemory without direct memory access. Detects a step-over breakpoint (0xCC) placed by the debugger at the return address.

combinations.name: Memory breakpoint detection (guard page)
combinations.description: Allocates an executable buffer containing 0xC3 (RET), marks it as a guard page (PAGE_GUARD), then jumps to it. OllyDbg and ImmunityDebugger implement memory breakpoints via guard pages -- their presence causes execution to reach the pushed 'debugger detected' address instead of the SEH handler.

combinations.name: Write-watch debugger detection
combinations.description: Allocates a MEM_WRITE_WATCH region and calls APIs that should not write to it. If GetWriteWatch reports unexpected writes, a debugger or API hook is probing the buffer (e.g. reading parameters passed to APIs).

combinations.name: Function patch detection (IsDebuggerPresent hook)
combinations.description: Reads the first bytes of kernel32!IsDebuggerPresent from another process and compares them to the current process. A mismatch reveals that the function was patched or hooked by a debugger (e.g. ScyllaHide). Windows libraries share the same base address across all processes in a session.

combinations.name: DbgBreakPoint patch (anti-attach)
combinations.description: Resolves ntdll!DbgBreakPoint via GetProcAddress, makes it writable, and patches the INT3 with RET (0xC3). Prevents the debugger from breaking in when attaching to the process -- the attach thread exits silently instead.

combinations.name: DbgUiRemoteBreakin patch (anti-attach / self-termination)
combinations.description: Resolves ntdll!DbgUiRemoteBreakin via GetProcAddress and patches it with a TerminateProcess call. Any attempt to attach a debugger to the process triggers immediate self-termination.

combinations.name: Self-debugging anti-attach
combinations.description: Spawns a child process that calls DebugActiveProcess on the parent. Failure (returns FALSE) reveals that another debugger is already attached, as only one debugger can be attached to a process at a time.

combinations.name: OutputDebugString GetLastError trick
combinations.description: Sets a known error code, calls OutputDebugStringA, then checks GetLastError. A debugger consuming the DBG_PRINTEXCEPTION_C event leaves the error code unchanged. Pre-Vista technique; ineffective against modern debuggers.

combinations.name: Parent process check (NtQueryInformationProcess)
combinations.description: Compares the parent process PID (from ProcessBasicInformation) against the PID of explorer.exe (obtained via GetShellWindow + GetWindowThreadProcessId). A different parent indicates the process was launched from a debugger.

combinations.name: Parent process check (Toolhelp32)
combinations.description: Iterates processes via Toolhelp32 to find the current process's parent PID and compare its name against 'explorer.exe'. A parent other than explorer.exe (e.g. a debugger or cmd.exe) triggers detection.

combinations.name: VEH instruction counting anti-debug
combinations.description: Registers a VEH to count EXCEPTION_SINGLE_STEP events triggered by hardware breakpoints set on a NOP sled. Under a debugger, single-step events are intercepted before the VEH, causing the counter to reach an unexpected value.

combinations.name: SwitchToThread anti-tracing
combinations.description: Loops calling SwitchToThread (wrapping NtYieldExecution). Under single-step tracing in a debugger, no context switch can occur and both return 0/STATUS_NO_YIELD_PERFORMED respectively. A counter reaching the expected value confirms tracing.

combinations.name: CreateFile process binary exclusive open
combinations.description: Attempts to exclusively open the current process's own executable file (GENERIC_READ, share=0). Fails if a debugger holds an unclosed handle from the CREATE_PROCESS_DEBUG_EVENT, revealing debugger presence.

combinations.name: LoadLibrary handle leak detection
combinations.description: Loads a DLL via LoadLibraryA then immediately tries to open it with exclusive access via CreateFileA. If the open fails, the debugger did not close the LOAD_DLL_DEBUG_INFO handle from the LOAD_DLL_DEBUG_EVENT.

combinations.name: Dynamic NTAPI resolution for anti-debug
combinations.description: Loads ntdll.dll at runtime and resolves native NT APIs (NtQueryInformationProcess, NtClose, NtSetInformationThread, etc.) via GetProcAddress. Bypasses static import analysis and hooks placed on Win32 wrappers.
